text: to Ronin . “What is there for me, in those memories?” He raised his free hand to his cheek and scratched. “Pain, loss, the reminder of all the terrible things I did and witnessed? If there was anyone in my life before, they’re gone now .” Lara cringed, and he turned his head to her and cupped her face . “What I have with you, Lara, has brought me more contentment than anything else. The memories I am making with you render everything before useless to me .” “What if there was someone you…you felt the same for as me, but just forgot about?” She clenched her fists in her lap . “If there was anyone I ever felt the same about, I would never have forgotten. You are burned into every circuit, every bit of data. You are part of my core function now .” She covered his hands with her own and leaned her forehead against his, closing her eyes. How had she come to need him, in so short a time? Ronin had worked his way into every part of her being. When he’d pushed her away, distanced himself because of his own pain, she’d felt it in her soul. She needed him, needed his solid, unwavering presence, his patience, his thoughtfulness. She craved his touch not just in passion, but in comfort . Tabitha leaving home had been painful, and her death had left a hole in Lara’s heart that would never heal . But she wasn’t sure she’d survive losing Ronin . “We left Cheyenne so we could be together.” She turned her head to look at Newton. “My sister went to live with a bot. Warlord killed them both. He gave Ronin a chance to prove himself, by killing me .” “We never hypothesized that the transferal of his consciousness would be the catalyst that eliminated his humanity,” Newton said softly. “That was never our intention .” “Can he be stopped ?” Newton nodded. “The same as anyone else .” “Then why hasn’t he been?” she demanded, fire sparking in her belly. All the suffering, fear, and death. “You turned him on. Why didn’t you turn him off ?” “Were he still human, and I merely the physician who treated him after a terrible injury, would you ask that question of me ?” “ Yes !” His metallic frown deepened, and his body sagged. “I am not a destroyer. My goal was to assist the world’s recovery, but that aim has failed utterly. In Cheyenne, especially, my intervention has only heightened people’s suffering. That’s why I’ve been here. Perhaps, at one point, I believed I could do it. After hearing rumors of the things he’d done… I am not designed to destroy, Lara. So, I’ve laid here for years, hiding from the world I wanted to help .” “Why not get someone else to help, then? Someone to do the dirty work? No, you just sat here, rusting away while he killed people. Not just humans, but everyone!” She glared at him, chest heaving, but the fire snuffed out as realization dawned. “And we just left them , too .” Hanging her head, she stabbed her fingers into her hair, tugging it back . Ronin wrapped his arms around her and pulled her into his embrace. His hold was gentle, but firm. “You know that’s not how we work. Not anymore than it is how you do .” “Bad humans get punished. Why not bots , too ?” “He has a small army around himself. Who could oppose that?” Ronin asked . “Other bots, like you.” She lifted her head to meet his eyes . “I’m only one. Even if I managed to walk up and deactivate him permanently, I’d never get out in one piece .” Lara flung her hand toward Newton. “He’s the fucking Prophet ! You recognized him. Wouldn’t others? There have to be other bots in Cheyenne who would oppose Warlord, and they’d trust Newton .” “The Prophet is a legend, Lara. I’m just a bot designed to assist in scientific research and experimentation .” “But they’d listen to you. You know things. You turned a lot of them on, didn’t you ?” Newton raised his hands, turning them to look at his scratched palms. “I turned them on, yes, but it was individuals like Warlord who provided them the things they required to function properly .” “ Ronin provides the things they need. Him and the other dustwalkers bring in the goods .” “That’s true,” Ronin said, “but it doesn’t matter. I’m still an outsider in Cheyenne, existing apart from the community he built .” “Built? Humans already built it, he just took it over!” Lara pulled away from him. How could she not feel guilty for leaving now, having learned all this? She hadn’t even said a word to Gary and Kate, who’d helped her despite having no obligation to do so, whose lives hung by a thread that Warlord could cut at any moment. “He’ll kill them all. Every single one of them. It’s just a matter of time. And you’d just sit here and do nothing . ” “What would you have me do?” Newton asked, meeting her gaze. “Are the three of us to walk into the center of town and attempt to rally robots to our cause? You know what he’s capable of, Lara. You’ve seen it firsthand .” “The humans would fight. We’ve got nothing else to lose. And what about the people in the base? If you told them what he’s doing, would they just sit back and wait until he eventually finds them ?” “We can’t go back to Cheyenne.” There was steel in Ronin’s voice. “It’s not safe for you .” “Is anywhere safe?” Lara closed her eyes and inhaled. Her throat was tight, and she was on the verge of tears. She was demanding a lot, pushing against things well beyond her control. Even if she hadn’t interacted with
